relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The final charge is spoken before Divine judgment, appearing, and reign.
Proclaim the word with readiness, patience, and teaching.
A season comes when healthy teaching is refused.
Truth is traded for myths.
Timothy remains sober and fulfills service.
Paul senses his departure near.
He has struggled the beautiful struggle and kept the trust.
A crown of justice awaits all who love the appearing.
Paul longs for Timothy to come quickly.
Demas leaves; others are sent onward.
Luke remains, and Mark is useful again.
Tychicus is sent to Ephesus.
Paul asks for cloak, scrolls, and parchments.
Harm done by Alexander is entrusted to the Lord's justice.
Timothy is warned to guard himself.
Abandoned at defense, Paul prays mercy over them.
The Lord stands by Paul and empowers the proclamation.
The Lord rescues and brings him into the heavenly reign.
Prisca, Aquila, and Onesiphorus' household are greeted.
Names and illnesses remain part of the real story.
Come before winter; the siblings greet you.
The Lord be with your spirit; grace be with you.

contemplative_note: 2 Timothy 4 is the final flame: proclamation with patience, the beautiful struggle completed, loneliness met by the Lord’s presence, and grace with the spirit.
